figured out why this is still failing and it exposes an edge-case that is not accounted for in the Scraper, which would be of clients having `http`-baseurls and serving data in extension-less files. `_httpfilelist()` looks for URLs at a directory's webpage [ending with the same extension as the pattern](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/blob/main/sunpy/net/scraper.py#L290) (like `.fits`, etc) before parsing it further. This wasn't a problem before since the source was ftp then and it's straightforward to get a list of files in a directory. i could work on fixing this if it'd be more convenient that way, let me know!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2204503300/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

The `deprecated` decorator (the one we use in sunpy) edits the documentation to include something like this:
```
    .. deprecated:: 3.1
        We will be moving this, sunpy.net.Scraper is where it's at now.
```

`Table.show_in_notebook()` method was deprecated in #15905 due to issues regarding the compatibility and maintainability of JS code in astropy with developments in Jupyter Notebook (JupyterLab frontend components got introduced in Notebook v7 onwards which broke the js code).

Alternative solutions were considered, namely [ipydatagrid](https://github.com/bloomberg/ipydatagrid), and [itables](https://github.com/mwouts/itables), and after some discussions on dependency management on the slack, my understanding is that the final consensus came down to deprecating it for the time being? I was wondering if adding back proper support (likely via the third-party Jupyter-datatable-package route) is still on the radar as people did seem to use it.

For additional context, this was something that came up when we were considering having searchable Tables over at sunpy and there's a chance that despite my best efforts I could still be missing some astropy context here. I'd be happy to work on a PR here upstream if this idea still seems good!","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16702/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16702/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/2223949026","html_url":"https://github.com/astropy/astropy/issues/16702#issuecomment-2223949026","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/16702","id":2223949026,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAB_CCc6EjsTi","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-07-11T21:13:06Z","updated_at":"2024-07-11T21:13:06Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Thanks for your response! I fully agree that having JS code like that in astropy is not at all ideal for a plethora of reasons.

> Or, if someone wants to support a different package, add a new hook like table.to_itables()?
> I'm fine with any of that, as long as the js code is not shipped in astropy itself

This is pretty much exactly what I'm trying to get at here. I think interactive tables directly in Notebook could be a nice feature to add back like this in a more sustainable and reliable manner (via an external package like ipytables). And I wanted to see if the idea sits well with the project development community. 

If it sounds good, I'll also be available to work on contributing this, though I could see myself needing a bit of help with design decisions down the line haha","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/astropy/astropy/issues/comments/2223949026/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

Added support for saving a map in  asdf file using .save 

here is an example 
` import astropy.units as u `
` import sunpy `
` import sunpy.data.sample ` 
` import sunpy.map `

` aia_map = sunpy.map.Map(sunpy.data.sample.AIA_171_IMAGE) `
` aia_map.save("map.asdf") `
inspired from https://docs.sunpy.org/en/stable/generated/gallery/saving_and_loading_data/genericmap_in_asdf.html
edit 
Added support for loading generic sunpy map with asdf extension  similar to fits file

here is an example 
` import astropy.units as u `
` import sunpy `
` import sunpy.data.sample ` 
` import sunpy.map `

` aia_map = sunpy.map.Map(sunpy.data.sample.AIA_171_IMAGE) `
` aia_map.save("map.asdf") `
inspired from https://docs.sunpy.org/en/stable/generated/gallery/saving_and_loading_data/genericmap_in_asdf.html
edit 
Added support for loading generic sunpy map with asdf extension  similar to fits file

Added support for saving a map in  asdf file using .save 

here is an example 
` import astropy.units as u `
` import sunpy `
` import sunpy.data.sample ` 
` import sunpy.map `

` aia_map = sunpy.map.Map(sunpy.data.sample.AIA_171_IMAGE) `
` aia_map.save("map.asdf") `
inspired from https://docs.sunpy.org/en/stable/generated/gallery/saving_and_loading_data/genericmap_in_asdf.html
edit 
Added support for loading generic sunpy map with asdf extension  similar to fits file

","user":{"login":"Sauravroy34","id":136881235,"node_id":"U_kgDOCCikUw","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/136881235?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Sauravroy34","html_url":"https://github.com/Sauravroy34","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Sauravroy34/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Sauravroy34/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Sauravroy34/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Sauravroy34/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Sauravroy34/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Sauravroy34/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Sauravroy34/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Sauravroy34/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Sauravroy34/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":" Added support for saving a map in  asdf file using .save 

here is an example 
` import astropy.units as u `
` import sunpy `
` import sunpy.data.sample ` 
` import sunpy.map `

` aia_map = sunpy.map.Map(sunpy.data.sample.AIA_171_IMAGE) `
` aia_map.save("map.asdf") `
inspired from https://docs.sunpy.org/en/stable/generated/gallery/saving_and_loading_data/genericmap_in_asdf.html
edit 
Added support for loading generic sunpy map with asdf extension  similar to fits file

{"action":"opened","number":7664,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/pulls/7664","id":1908211057,"node_id":"PR_kwDOACEKh85xvP1x","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7664","diff_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7664.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7664.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7664","number":7664,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Add backwards compatibility for the scraper","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Aims to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.
- *also renamed `timepattern` to `dt_pattern` since it's also appearing in docs rn, felt it more fitting.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.
- *also renamed `timepattern` to `dt_pattern` since it's also appearing in docs rn, felt it more fitting.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.
- *also renamed `timepattern` to `dt_pattern` since it's also appearing in docs rn, felt it more fitting.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far :cowboy_hat_face:","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7664/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7664/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2179428084","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7664#issuecomment-2179428084","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7664","id":2179428084,"node_id":"IC_kwDOACEKh86B5270","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-19T20:36:47Z","updated_at":"2024-06-19T20:36:47Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"Thanks for your reviews!

A guide for writing clients with the newer format is added in the all-things-scraper-clients / extending-Fido [topic-guide](https://github.com/exitflynn/sunpy/blob/scraper_rewrite/docs/topic_guide/extending_fido.rst) but it's not explicitly about migrating yet. Does what's there work or should I try and address migration more?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/comments/2179428084/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

scraper","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Aims to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.
- *also renamed `timepattern` to `dt_pattern` since it's also appearing in docs rn, felt it more fitting.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.
- *also renamed `timepattern` to `dt_pattern` since it's also appearing in docs rn, felt it more fitting.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far scraper","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Aims to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.
- *also renamed `timepattern` to `dt_pattern` since it's also appearing in docs rn, felt it more fitting.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far scraper","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Aims to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.
- *also renamed `timepattern` to `dt_pattern` since it's also appearing in docs rn, felt it more fitting.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far scraper","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Aims to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.
- *also renamed `timepattern` to `dt_pattern` since it's also appearing in docs rn, felt it more fitting.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far  * A class method :meth:`~sunpy.net.base_client.BaseClient.register_values`; this registers the "attrs" that are supported by the client.
   It returns a dictionary where keys are the supported attrs and values are lists of tuples.
   Each `tuple` contains the "attr" value and its description.
-* A class attribute ``pattern``; this is a string used to match the URLs supported by the client and extract metadata from the matched URLs.
-  The time and other metadata attributes for extraction are written using the `~sunpy.extern.parse.parse` format, using double curly-brackets so to differentiate them from ``kwargs`` parameters which are written in single curly-brackets.
+* A class attribute ``pattern``; this is a string used to match all URLs supported by the client and extract necessary metadata from the matched URLs.
+  The time and other metadata attributes for extraction are written within double curly-braces ``{{}}`` in a `parse <https://github.com/r1chardj0n3s/parse/>`__ format.
+  Regular placeholders for Python format strings can still be included via single curly braces ``{}`` with their respective parameters passed as the ``kwargs``.
+  An example of how such a pattern looks like is given in the algorithm explanation below.
+
+Note: The scraper has supported regex-based patterns for a long time, which is what it will still expect by default. However that is in the process of being replaced with parse-style patterns in the future versions of the package. To use the newer parse-style patterns, it's currently required to pass it as the ``format`` argument to the scraper.
 
 Each such client relies on the `~sunpy.net.scraper.Scraper` to be able to query for files using the :meth:`~sunpy.net.scraper.Scraper.filelist` method. The general algorithm to explain how the `~sunpy.net.scraper.Scraper` is able to do this is:
+A brief explanation of how the Scraper works is as follows:
+
+1. Drop the filename from the pattern and generate a list of directories to search for files.
+2. For each directory, get a list of files present in it.
+3. For each file, check if it matches the pattern.
+4. For each file that matches the pattern, check if it is in the timerange. If it is, add it to the output.
+
+For a more verbose in-depth explanation on how this is accomplished internally:
 
-1. It takes as input a generalised ``pattern`` of how a desired filepath looks like, following the ``parse`` format. A version of the pattern following the datetime format is also generated, called the ``timepattern``.
+1. A Scraper object takes as input the generalised ``pattern`` of how a desired filepath looks like, in the ``parse`` format. Upon initialisation, a version of the pattern following the datetime format is also internally generated, called the ``dt_pattern``.
 
 .. code-block:: python
 
     >>> from sunpy.net import Scraper
     >>> pattern = ('http://proba2.oma.be/{instrument}/data/bsd/{{year:4d}}/{{month:2d}}/{{day:2d}}/'
     ...            '{instrument}_lv1_{{year:4d}}{{month:2d}}{{day:2d}}_{{hour:2d}}{{minute:2d}}{{second:2d}}.fits')
-    >>> s = Scraper(pattern, instrument='swap')
+    >>> s = Scraper(format=pattern, instrument='swap')
+    >>> to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.
- *also renamed `timepattern` to `dt_pattern` since it's also appearing in docs rn, felt it more fitting.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far scraper","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Aims to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.
- *also renamed `timepattern` to `dt_pattern` since it's also appearing in docs rn, felt it more fitting.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far Needed","color":"e99695","default":false,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2024-06-06T23:40:53Z","updated_at":"2024-06-26T22:15:53Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/pulls/7664","html_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7664","diff_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7664.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7664.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Aims to add backwards compatibility for the base scraper post-rewrite from #7077 so that we can go through a deprecation period for the breaking changes.

Addressing @dstansby's [suggestions](https://github.com/sunpy/sunpy/pull/7227#pullrequestreview-1682939171) on how we can approach this, so far this PR pretty much addresses all the points in terms of the Scraper design, namely the PR:

  - Makes `pattern` optional (ie. , pattern=None)
  - Adds a new keyword-only argument, `format`, to take the new format
  - Raises an error if both pattern and format are provided
  - Uses old behaviour, and raises a deprecation warning saying to use new format=... instead if only `pattern` passed
  - Uses new behaviour if only `format` passed.

Outside of changing `sunpy.net.scraper` I've done the following:
- Added back the tests for the original scraper with the ones I wrote (distinguished by a `new_format` suffix) and they all should be passing now.
- During the rewrite, I had previously moved out `check_timerange` from `scraper` to `scraper_utils` but I moved it back in so long as the old scraper is still around since I felt it'd get a bit too complex otherwise (for example, it'd require internal scraper functions that were removed in the rewrite, etc).
- Updated hopefully all the relevant docstring examples and the base dataretriever clients' code. I am _not_ sure of all the tests passing, i'm curious of the CI's verdict on that rn :P
- I've also updated the `extending_fido.rst` doc about `pattern` vs `format` and have tried to improve some of the documentation I wrote previously explaining the Scraper algorithm.
- *also renamed `timepattern` to `dt_pattern` since it's also appearing in docs rn, felt it more fitting.

The really long file for the scraper tests right now make me doubt a little if maybe I should've skimmed over some tests when adding backwards compatibility, I'd love to know if I've been on the right path with this so far The way it's handled in main is we simply just don't use `Path()` to get OS-specific filepaths, and just linux style forward-slash strings. I've reverted it to match with main, though a discussion could be had if we should include `pathlib.Path` generated paths there 🤔 \r\n\r\nAnother error was because in one of the functions, scraper internally replaces \\\\ with / in the pattern when in windows. I had previously replaced one of the two pattern variables and had missed the other one things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073

things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 clients","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"as things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 clients","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"as things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 clients","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"as things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 +def check_timerange(pattern, url, timerange):
+    """
+    Checks whether the time range represented in *url* intersects
+    with the given time range.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    url : `str`
+        URL of the file.
+    timerange : `~sunpy.time.TimeRange`
+        Time interval for which files were searched.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    `bool`
+        `True` if URL's valid time range overlaps the given timerange, else `False`.
+    """
+    exdict = parse(pattern, url).named
+    if exdict['year'] < 100:
+        exdict['year'] = 2000 + exdict['year']
+    if 'month' not in exdict:
+                if 'month_name' in exdict:
+                    exdict['month'] = datetime.strptime(exdict['month_name'], '%B').month
+                elif 'month_name_abbr' in exdict:
+                    exdict['month'] = datetime.strptime(exdict['month_name_abbr'], '%b').month
+    tr = get_timerange_from_exdict(exdict)
+    return tr.intersects(timerange)
+
+def smaller_pattern(directoryPattern):
+    """
+    Obtain the smaller time step for the given pattern.
+    """
+    if "%S" in directoryPattern:
+        return relativedelta(seconds=1)
+    elif "%M" in directoryPattern:
+        return relativedelta(minutes=1)
+    elif any(hour in directoryPattern for hour in ["%H"]):
+        return relativedelta(hours=1)
+    elif any(day in directoryPattern for day in ["%d", "%j"]):
+        return relativedelta(days=1)
+    elif any(month in directoryPattern for month in ["%b", "%B", "%m"]):
+        return relativedelta(months=1)
+    elif any(year in directoryPattern for year in ["%Y", "%y"]):
+        return relativedelta(years=1)
+    else:
+        return None
+
+def date_floor(date, timestep):
+    """
+    Return the "floor" of the given date and time step.
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    datetime : `datetime.datetime` or `astropy.time.Time`
+        The date to floor
+    timestep : `dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta`
+        The smallest time step to floor
+    Returns
+    -------
+    `datetime.datetime`
+        The time floored at the given time step
+    """
+    date_parts = [int(p) for p in date.strftime('%Y,%m,%d,%H,%M,%S').split(',')]
+    date_parts[-1] = date_parts[-1] % 60
+    date = datetime(*date_parts)
+    orig_time_tup = date.timetuple()
+    time_tup = [orig_time_tup.tm_year, orig_time_tup.tm_mon, orig_time_tup.tm_mday,
+                orig_time_tup.tm_hour, orig_time_tup.tm_min, orig_time_tup.tm_sec]
+    if timestep == relativedelta(minutes=1):
+        time_tup[-1] = 0
+    elif timestep == relativedelta(hours=1):
+        time_tup[-2:] = [0, 0]
+    elif timestep == relativedelta(days=1):
+        time_tup[-3:] = [0, 0, 0]
+    elif timestep == relativedelta(months=1):
+        time_tup[-4:] = [1, 0, 0, 0]
+    elif timestep == relativedelta(years=1):
+        time_tup[-5:] = [1, 1, 0, 0, 0]
+
+    return datetime(*time_tup)
+

clients","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"as things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

clients","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"as things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 This is something I brought up on the matrix chat before, and would be fixed by #7077. I've added it here to keep track of it better.
For the reproduction, let's suppose we make a scraper client with '%Y.fits', it's `exdict` would look like `{"year":2014}`
```python
from sunpy.net.scraper import Scraper
s0 = Scraper('%Y.fits')
s0._check_timerange('2014.fits', TimeRange("2015-01-01", "2015-01-02")) # True
# since end time here is 2015 01 01 00 00 00
```
```python
from sunpy.net.scraper import Scraper, get_timerange_from_exdict
from sunpy.time import TimeRange

tr = get_timerange_from_exdict({"year":2014})
tr.intersects(TimeRange("2015-01-01", "2015-01-02")) # False
# since end time here is 2014 31 12 59 59 59
```
One would expect both ways to work the same way. In particular, the handling of timeranges in the first one seems weird so it was decided that the intended behaviour to be the second one.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7174/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/sunpy/sunpy/issues/7174/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 

This PR is part of my Google Summer of Code 2023 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 

This PR is part of my Google Summer of Code 2023 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 

This PR is part of my Google Summer of Code 2023 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 

This PR is part of my Google Summer of Code 2023 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 

This PR is part of my Google Summer of Code 2023 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073

clients","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"as things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 clients","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"as things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 clients","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"as things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 clients","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"as things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 clients","user":{"login":"exitflynn","id":37214399,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM3MjE0Mzk5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/37214399?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn","html_url":"https://github.com/exitflynn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/exitflynn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"as things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073 things are right now, while defining scraper-based dataretriever clients, the `baseurl` which we send to scraper, is a pattern in the strftime format and we also require a second `pattern` string which is the same thing but formatted in the `parse` format.
What this PR does is change this to requiring only one full parse-formatted `pattern` in the scraper and generating the strftime formatted `timepattern` from it.

Having the new parse-format `pattern` around will also help considerably help simplifying many existing functions like `_extractDateURL()` which are using regex for parsing and help with #4888.

closes #7073
